response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang background, but the resume shows minimal Java exposure (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling years ago) and no Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience at all. The single relevant data point for IDE internals is the short stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ for LabWindows/CVI over a decade ago, which doesn't transfer to JVM language tooling. Core JD requirements around deep JVM expertise and the Spring ecosystem are not met.
strengths: Brief prior experience contributing to an IDE product (LabWindows/CVI) at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship background — TDD, CI/CD, testing, developer tooling, workshops
Demonstrated ability to work on complex systems across web, embedded, and low-latency microservices
concerns: No demonstrable Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java use, far below required JVM depth
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience listed in any role
Recent career trajectory is centered on TypeScript/Node and AI tooling, not JVM language tooling or IDE internals

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience
